images.altText: An old man in a suit with medals on his chest waves an Australian flag
Women wearing medals place their heads together
Shadow of soldier playing last post reflected on wall

children.content: Australians paid tribute to those who have served at services and marches, but the solemn mood at Melbourne, Sydney and Perth Dawn Services was interrupted by boos during Welcome to Country addresses. Follow live.

items.title: Australians mark Anzac Day in solemn memorials as individuals heckling at services condemned

updates.title: The Melbourne man who confronted dawn service hecklers
'No-one wins a war': Veteran reflects on Anzac Day

items.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

items.title: Canberrans with disability fear NDIS changes will cut vital support

children.content: Canberrans living with disability warn planned changes to the NDIS could strip back support, undermining their mental health and sense of certainty.

image.altText: A woman sitting on a couch looking at the camera.

items.title: Payroll error leaves thousands of hospital staff waiting for fortnightly wages

children.content: Thousands of North Canberra Hospital employees were not paid on time this fortnight, with some staff raising concerns on social media that they had received pay slips but no corresponding bank deposits, after a payroll processing issue.

image.altText: North Canberra Hospital exterior

items.title: 'Not what the community wanted': Big Splash lease spared despite backlash

children.content: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

gallery.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

thumbnail.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: 'Concerning': Police reveal DNA links same man to two alleged incidents

children.content: Two separate incidents nearly a year apart in Canberra’s south appeared unrelated until ACT Policing used DNA analysis to link them to the same man.

image.altText: A drawing of a man with dark hair and beard.

items.title: Rattenbury denies quitting over coalition talks, but they can't have helped

children.content: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a blazer stands outdoors looking serious.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

gallery.altText: Soccer players wearing green, black and white uniforms smile at each other during a game

thumbnail.altText: Soccer players wearing green, black and white uniforms smile at each other during a game

items.title: Pickett takes mark of the year contender in Demon's stunning outing

children.content: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

image.altText: Melbourne Demons' Kysaiah Pickett jumps into a Richmond player to take a mark in an AFL game.

items.title: Court shown Rebel Wilson text exchange about actor's bath incident

children.content: A Sydney court is shown a text message exchange between Hollywood star Rebel Wilson and a movie producer over a bathroom incident involving a young actor.

image.altText: Charlotte MacInnes in a white shirt and silver jewellery

items.title: What happens after you buy a house?

children.content: You've heard it all before: it's harder to buy a house.
But, if you do manage to get your foot on the property ladder, things are also more difficult afterwards.
The housing crisis has turned into an intergenerational wealth crisis, set to make paying your mortgage in retirement the norm.
So how did we get there? And how do we fix it?

image.altText: stylised image of a man and woman walking in front of a suburban home

gallery.altText: stylised image of a man and woman walking in front of a suburban home

thumbnail.altText: stylised image of a man and woman walking in front of a suburban home

items.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection

children.content: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

image.altText: Foot with big toe covered in white bandage

items.title: AI traffic fines set to net $440m in driver fines across Perth and WA

children.content: The cameras have handed out around 184,000 infringements since they started issuing fines in October last year, according to the WA government.

image.altText: A scan of an infringement notice

items.title: 'People don't understand' how vital Walers were in WWI, horse trainer says

children.content: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

articles.title: Which states get a public holiday for Anzac Day?

children.content: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

image.altText: poppies on a cenotaph

articles.title: VC recipient calls for federal government to step up veteran support

children.content: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers has called for a national rethink on the care of veterans and their families.

image.altText: A man holds a portrait of a young man in military uniform

items.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care

children.content: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

image.altText: A woman with blue eyes and short blonde hair smiles. She is outside.

items.title: Plan to end NDIS 'wild west' will flip rules on their head for providers

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will end the practice of handwritten invoices, providers without qualifications and a "let it rip" market of profiteering operators. But the sector warns changes must be navigated carefully.

image.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

gallery.altText: A red and black robot on a street with barriers in the background and a ribbon falling to the ground.

thumbnail.altText: A red and black robot on a street with barriers in the background and a ribbon falling to the ground.

items.title: The consequences of India's push to get more women in parliament

children.content: India's government proposed a plan to get more women into parliament but it came with one major condition.

image.altText: A crowd of protesters gather around a man in a black t-shirt burning a piece of paper

items.title: The 10 books that knocked our critics' socks off this month

children.content: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

image.altText: Ten book covers on a blue background.

items.title: Pentagon awards $276m AUKUS contract funded by Australia

children.content: The US Department of Defense announces the first major submarine contract to deliver on the AUKUS security deal.

gallery.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

thumbnail.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

items.title: Jane Fonda was in her 40s when she changed the way we exercise

children.content: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

image.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

items.title: Norway and Türkiye follow Australia with teen social media bans

children.content: Several European nations seek to rein in children's use of social media after Australia introduced its ban last December.

image.altText: Hands holding a phone with some social media platforms visible on screen.

items.title: UK's generational smoking ban applauded in Australia

children.content: Anyone born after 2008 will be banned from buying cigarettes or vapes for life in the UK when a new bill becomes law.

image.altText: An unidentifiable person's hands holding an unlit cigarette and a lit red lighter.

items.title: Bail denied for woman accused of repeatedly trying to kill ex-partner

children.content: The accused's lawyers applied for her release on bail last year on the basis that she had "dental and medical issues" that could not be managed in prison.

image.altText: A woman sitting at a desk in front of a bookcase

items.title: Oyster growers enduring 'death by a thousand cuts' after being closed again

children.content: Oyster growing areas on the Yorke Peninsula are ordered to close due to the detection of a toxic phytoplankton for the second time this year.

gallery.altText: A man standing waist deep in water

thumbnail.altText: A man standing waist deep in water

items.title: Man, 72, charged with decade-old murder of football great's brother

children.content: Victoria Police charge a Colac man with murder as part of its investigation into the 2012 suspicious disappearance of St Kilda football champion Barry Breen's brother, Brendan Breen.

image.altText: Two photo of Brendan Breen, one in which he is posing for what looks like a police photo and another of him smiling.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms Anzac Day plans

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms he will be attending Anzac Day services tomorrow, saying he intends to pay his respects, one week on from his release on bail.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith is seen during a break the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney

items.title: Mapping the five other shipping routes critical for the global economy

children.content: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

gallery.altText: A map of different international waterways across the world.

thumbnail.altText: A map of different international waterways across the world.

items.title: Petrol stations fined for mismatched pricing, misleading consumers

children.content: NSW Fair Trading issues more than 200 fines to petrol stations found to have breached pricing rules. Meanwhile, motorists celebrate a drop in prices as the premier weighs future fuel security measures.

image.altText: a hand holding a mobile phone with the fuel check app open

items.title: Australia is searching for productivity. Could reducing inequality be the answer?

children.content: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

image.altText: Unidentifiable kids in a classroom.

items.title: Michael Tilson Thomas, 12-time Grammy-winning conductor, dies aged 81

children.content: The celebrated conductor led the San Francisco Symphony for 25 years, and co-founded the New World Symphony.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of Michael Tilson Thomas conducting with both hands, one hold a baton, raised in front of him.
Michael Tilson Thomas conducts an orchestra with the audience in the background. Both arms are flung in the air.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of Michael Tilson Thomas conducting with both hands, one hold a baton, raised in front of him.
Michael Tilson Thomas conducts an orchestra with the audience in the background. Both arms are flung in the air.

items.title: Sniper who killed top bikie in public execution revealed after gag on identity lifted

children.content: The former soldier who publicly assassinated ex-Rebels bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex in 2020 can be publicly identified for the first time, after he successfully overturned a court order suppressing his identity.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a cap and white shirt on the right poses in a selfie with a quokka on the left.

items.title: Resigning mayor makes plea for 'kindness', 'respect' from councillors

children.content: A greater Hobart mayor will resign after serving in a public role for three decades, saying 'keyboard warriors' and a 'toxic culture' from councillors prompted her decision.

image.altText: Paula Wriedt steps down as Kingborough mayor

items.title: Hundreds of fishing boats sit idle off southern China — but they're not there to fish

children.content: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

image.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

items.title: Blues star says Women's Origin timing 'not ideal' after six-month lay-off

children.content: Kezie Apps is one of a number of Origin stars who will play her first match in a matter of months when she represents the Blues, and she doesn't believe the lay-off is the best preparation.

image.altText: Kezie Apps holds the football while being tackled
